question.text: The credit risk in futures contracts is eliminated because:

answer.text: The clearing corporation becomes the central counter-party to all buyers and sellers
Both parties deposit the full contract value upfront
The government guarantees all futures transactions
Forward contract terms are adopted for settlement

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The clearing corporation of the exchange becomes the central counter-party (CCP) to all buyers and sellers, thereby guaranteeing settlement of trades. **The Trap:** Full value is NOT deposited — only margins (a fraction). Government doesn't guarantee trades. Forward terms are not used for futures settlement. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VII (Securities Operations) covers clearing corporation functions in detail. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The CCP guarantee makes exchange-traded derivatives fundamentally safer than OTC — this is why SEBI mandated clearing corporation settlement for all exchange trades.
